titles.en: Chapter on the Inheritance of a Child Whose Father Disowns Him After Previously Acknowledging Him
Chapter on the Inheritance of a Child Whose Father Disowns Him After Previously Acknowledging Him

segments: : "أَيُّمَا رَجُلٍ أَقَرَّ بِوَلَدِهِ ثُمَّ اِنْتَفَى مِنْهُ فَلَيْسَ لَهُ ذَلِكَ وَ لاَ كَرَامَةَ يُلْحَقُ بِهِ وَلَدُهُ إِذَا كَانَ مِنِ اِمْرَأَتِهِ أَوْ وَلِيدَتِهِ".
arabic.text: : "أَيُّمَا رَجُلٍ أَقَرَّ بِوَلَدِهِ ثُمَّ اِنْتَفَى مِنْهُ فَلَيْسَ لَهُ ذَلِكَ وَ لاَ كَرَامَةَ يُلْحَقُ بِهِ وَلَدُهُ إِذَا كَانَ مِنِ اِمْرَأَتِهِ أَوْ وَلِيدَتِهِ".

segments:
Hadith.5680 - Hammad narrated from Al-Halabi, from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
"Any man who acknowledges his child and then later denies him has no right to do so, and he deserves no respect.
His child will be attributed to him if the child is from his wife or his maidservant."
en.bab-ul-qaim-publications.text:
Hadith.5680 - Hammad narrated from Al-Halabi, from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
"Any man who acknowledges his child and then later denies him has no right to do so, and he deserves no respect.
His child will be attributed to him if the child is from his wife or his maidservant."

narratorChain.raw: 5680 - رَوَى حَمَّادٌ عَنِ اَلْحَلَبِيِّ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ
